text.title: String Externalization — Keys, ICU Format, and Python Babel
text.summary: Dotted key naming convention, ICU MessageFormat for plurals/select, Python Babel setup, and FastAPI locale middleware.

rule.statement: Use stable dotted keys (feature.surface.intent) for all user-facing strings; never auto-generate hash keys or use the EN string as the key.
rule.rationale: Stable keys survive copy edits; hash keys change when content changes, invalidating all translations. String-as-key forces re-translation on wording tweaks.

rule.statement: Use ICU MessageFormat for all plurals and gender/select — never gettext-style ngettext for new code; emit all CLDR-required plural categories for the target locale.
rule.rationale: EN has only one/other; Ukrainian requires one/few/many/other; Arabic requires zero/one/two/few/many/other. Missing categories produce wrong strings for specific numbers.

antipattern.description: Emitting only one/other plural categories for Ukrainian, Polish, Russian, or Arabic.
antipattern.reason: These languages require additional CLDR plural categories; emitting only one/other produces grammatically wrong strings for numbers like 2, 3, 11, 21.

rule.statement: Use pybabel extract to pull strings into messages.pot; compile to binary .mo files before deployment — gettext only reads compiled catalogues.
rule.rationale: Shipping uncompiled .po files means the translation layer silently falls back to EN; the compile step must be part of the build, not manual.

example.code: pybabel extract -F babel.cfg -o locales/messages.pot . pybabel update -i locales/messages.pot -d locales pybabel compile -d locales

rule.statement: Detect locale from request in priority order: query param, Accept-Language header, user preference; store in a ContextVar so all downstream code reads the correct locale without passing it explicitly.
rule.rationale: Passing locale as a function parameter through every layer is fragile and verbose; ContextVar provides an implicit request-scoped locale that is safe for async frameworks.
